text: Passing --model-think or --model-work without --model-mode auto should fail loudly (non-zero exit, clear message), not be silently accepted and ignored. Today scaffold-workflow.cjs exits 0 and the flags are no-ops in default mode, which hides operator mistakes. A check should assert that `--model-think fable` WITHOUT `--model-mode auto` exits non-zero.

In --model-mode auto, every think-class agent() call (Brief, RED, PathGap, CodeGate, Review, TestGate, DocsGate, Writeup) should also carry effort: 'high'; mechanical and work phases must NOT get an effort opt. Today auto emits a model: opt but never an effort: opt (grep of a generated --profile delivery --model-mode auto script shows zero `effort:` occurrences).

The generator's stdout summary should report the active model mode so the operator sees it at a glance. Today scaffold-workflow.cjs prints the phase order and launch-readiness but never states whether --model-mode is default or auto (nor the resolved think/work model picks). A check should assert that running with `--model-mode auto` prints a line mentioning the mode.

Flag-validation errors should echo the offending value to speed debugging. Today `--model-mode bogus` prints `ERROR: --model-mode must be 'default' or 'auto'` without showing `bogus`, and `--model-think gpt` lists the allowed set without showing `gpt`. A check should assert the error output for an invalid value contains that value.

Expected: when triage judges a goal decline-tier, conductor emits a cost/overkill caveat and offers inline execution before generating+launching, even when explicitly invoked. Actual: it generated+launched a full run on a 4-task single-skill change (about $45 est.) with no enforced pre-launch caveat.
suggestedCriterion: conductor SKILL.md triage (step 1b) contains an explicit branch for 'explicitly invoked but still decline/overkill tier' that requires surfacing a cost/overkill warning + offering inline execution before scaffold/launch.

text: conductor SKILL.md reads like a book (2410 words, over skill-lint's 1200-word flag). Relocate the heaviest section — the 945-word '2. Generate the conductor' generator-flag reference — into references/ (progressive disclosure) until the body clears skill-lint's flag book flag. Do NOT delete meaning; move it (the floor's content-anchor test enforces that load-bearing rules stay documented somewhere in the skill).

Expected: a conductor run carries a verifiable definition of done. scaffold-workflow.cjs accepts --dod-file <json> ({"criteria":[{"id","text","tier":"checkable"|"judged","check"?}]} — check required iff checkable; unreadable/malformed/empty files and bad tiers rejected with exit 1), bakes DOD_CRITERIA into the emitted script, emits a DoDBaseline phase (pre-work measurement of checkable criteria; omitted when all criteria are judged) and a DoDGate phase (before Writeup/PR: verify every criterion — checkable = run its command in the worktree, exit 0 = MET; judged = evidence-cited MET/UNMET/PARTIAL — then fix-loop <=3 rounds and hard-fail), persists dod:{criteria:DOD_CRITERIA} in the checkpoint payload, and instructs the PR worker to include a 'Definition of done' scorecard in the PR body. --profile lite/delivery REQUIRE --dod-file (or an explicit --no-dod); --no-dod suppresses the gate. scripts/test-scaffold.cjs (editable surface) must be extended to cover all of this and stay green. Actual: no goal-met meter exists — a run can pass every gate, open a PR, and still not have done what was asked. Exact implementation guidance (code-complete): Task 1 of docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-10-dod-and-panel-review.md in the MAIN checkout /Users/liemlhd/Documents/git/Personal/lirbox (gitignored — read via that absolute path, not the worktree).
suggestedCriterion: scaffolding a --pr run with a mixed checkable+judged dod file yields a script where DoDBaseline precedes the work phases, DoDGate precedes Writeup, DOD_CRITERIA is baked in verbatim, the checkpoint payload contains dod:{criteria:DOD_CRITERIA}, the PR prompt contains 'Definition of done', and node --check passes; a judged-only dod emits no DoDBaseline; --profile lite without --dod-file exits non-zero; a checkable criterion without a check command exits non-zero.

text: Expected: under --profile delivery (default ON) or --review-panel, the CodeGate becomes a multi-agent panel driven by the conductor JS (fan-out via parallel(); the lead is a worker, NEVER an agent spawning agents): (1) a cheap diff-guard worker — docs/config-only change => gate records skipped and passes; (2) 4 parallel READ-ONLY dimension reviewers with a findings schema — bugs, security, reuse, conventions — plus a 5th git-history dimension under delivery only; (3) deterministic file:line dedup in JS; (4) per-finding confidence scorers using the /code-review 0-100 rubric verbatim, findings with confidence < 80 dropped; (5) a lead adjudicator+fixer (default agentType lirbox:lirbox-code-reviewer, overridable via --agent-code) that fixes every Critical/High, loops <=3 rounds, hard-fails on unresolved Critical/High, and records panel:{raw,deduped,confirmed} in results.codeGate. --no-review-panel reverts delivery to the single review+fix agent; the collapsed Review phase (lite / --merge-gates) always stays single-agent. Actual: CodeGate is one review+fix agent — no independent dimensions, no false-positive filtering. Exact implementation guidance: Task 2 of docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-10-dod-and-panel-review.md in the MAIN checkout /Users/liemlhd/Documents/git/Personal/lirbox (gitignored — read via absolute path).
suggestedCriterion: a delivery scaffold contains codegate:guard, const DIMENSIONS = [ with a history entry, a confidence >= 80 filter, and a codegate:lead-r loop carrying agentType lirbox:lirbox-code-reviewer; --review-panel with --enforce-code (non-delivery) emits the panel WITHOUT the history dimension; --no-review-panel under delivery and plain lite emit no DIMENSIONS; node --check passes on all of them.

text: Expected: workflow-report.cjs renders a '## Definition of done — n/m MET' table (criterion id+text, tier, verdict, evidence, baseline status — flagging a criterion already met pre-work as non-discriminating) from state.dod.criteria joined with results.dodGate.criteria and results.dodBaseline.baselines, plus a one-line code-review panel summary (raw -> deduped -> confirmed) from results.codeGate.panel; both sections absent for runs without a DoD/panel. Actual: the run report has no DoD or panel section. Exact implementation guidance: Task 3 of docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-10-dod-and-panel-review.md in the MAIN checkout /Users/liemlhd/Documents/git/Personal/lirbox (gitignored — read via absolute path).
suggestedCriterion: running workflow-report.cjs against a synthetic .workflows/state/<name>.json (dod with 2 criteria, dodGate verdicts MET+UNMET, dodBaseline ac1=met, codeGate.panel {raw:7,deduped:5,confirmed:2}) produces output containing 'Definition of done — 1/2 MET', 'already met pre-work', and '7 raw finding(s) → 5 deduped → 2 confirmed'.

text: Expected: conductor's SKILL.md documents a triage step 'Acquire the DoD' between triage and generate — source precedence (1 ticket/plan ACs refined to checkable form, never dropped; 2 a plan-check report's <script type="application/json" id="dod"> block; 3 derived from a bare goal), confirm-once via ONE AskUserQuestion (accept/edit), freeze to .workflows/<name>.dod.json passed as --dod-file, the 3-7-criteria guidance with NO hard cap, and the >10-ACs split proposal (independently-shippable slices, run 2 starts only after run 1's PR merges — never stacked branches; deferred slice recorded in run 1's state). references/generator-flags.md documents --dod-file/--no-dod (incl. the lite/delivery requirement and the DoDBaseline/DoDGate phases) and --review-panel/--no-review-panel (incl. lite staying single-agent). Actual: neither file mentions a DoD or the panel. Exact text: Task 4 of docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-10-dod-and-panel-review.md in the MAIN checkout /Users/liemlhd/Documents/git/Personal/lirbox (gitignored — read via absolute path).
suggestedCriterion: grep-based: SKILL.md contains 'Acquire the DoD', 'id="dod"', '--dod-file', and a split-proposal mention; references/generator-flags.md contains '--dod-file', '--no-dod', '--review-panel', 'DoDGate'.
